channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 4 things to know about the new sunscreen ingredient the FDA approved
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration approved a new sunscreen ingredient in the U.S. for the first time in 20 years. It's been used for decades in Europe and Asia.
item.content:encoded:
The Food and Drug Administration approved a new sunscreen ingredient in the U.S. for the first time in 20 years. It's been used for decades in Europe and Asia.

(Image credit: mihailomilovanovic/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: A plan to get lifesaving food to hungry kids was working well -- until it wasn't
item.description: Through an innovative program, parents in Senegal had easy access to a therapeutic food that's a boon for malnourished kids. Now there are shortages. Health specialists say U.S. aid cuts are to blame.
item.content:encoded:
Through an innovative program, parents in Senegal had easy access to a therapeutic food that's a boon for malnourished kids. Now there are shortages. Health specialists say U.S. aid cuts are to blame.

(Image credit: Ricci Shryock for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: 'Cool Ladies Club' is directed by 10 working-class women. They live up to the title
item.description: They gave smartphones to 10 women from a working-class Indian community to make a documentary about their unseen and unheralded lives. The results are .... pretty cool.
item.content:encoded:
They gave smartphones to 10 women from a working-class Indian community to make a documentary about their unseen and unheralded lives. The results are .... pretty cool.

(Image credit: Mangesh Gudekar/School of Media and Cultural Studies, TISS.
)
item.dc:creator: Neha Bhatt

item.title: COMIC: How excessive heat kills and how to stay safe
item.description: Human bodies have a natural cooling system, but it can do only so much in high temperatures and humidity. Here's the science behind how heat kills. And how to protect yourself.
item.content:encoded: Human bodies have a natural cooling system, but it can do only so much in high temperatures and humidity. Here's the science behind how heat kills. And how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: Pope Leo XIV's flight home from Spain was grounded so the king came to his aid
item.description: Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.
item.content:encoded:
Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.

(Image credit: Vatican Media via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says U.S. military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang
item.description: President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Gene Shalit, longtime 'Today' show movie critic, dies at 100
item.description: Known for his puffy hair, oversized handlebar mustache and a love for puns, Gene Shalit joined Today in 1970 and became arts editor in 1973. He was a middle-of-the-road critic, known for his wit and intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
Known for his puffy hair, oversized handlebar mustache and a love for puns, Gene Shalit joined Today in 1970 and became arts editor in 1973. He was a middle-of-the-road critic, known for his wit and intelligence.

(Image credit: Richard Drew)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Workers begin removing Trump's name from the Kennedy Center
item.description: Workers began removing President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday, hours after a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.
item.content:encoded:
Workers began removing President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday, hours after a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.

(Image credit: Cliff Owen)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Paramount-Warner Brothers merger gets Justice Department approval
item.description: The Justice Department closed its investigation into the proposed $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Brothers Discovery, saying it found no threat to competition or consumers.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department closed its investigation into the proposed $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Brothers Discovery, saying it found no threat to competition or consumers.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Justice Dept. approves Paramount's acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery
item.description: The decision paves the way for a proposed $111 billion merger uniting two rival studio giants — Paramount, owner of CBS, and the much larger Warner, which includes HBO and CNN.
item.content:encoded:
The decision paves the way for a proposed $111 billion merger uniting two rival studio giants — Paramount, owner of CBS, and the much larger Warner, which includes HBO and CNN.

(Image credit: Nick Ut)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik
